commands.review_summary:
The branch filters plugin-tools MCP exposure through a universal dangerous-tool deny list with gateway.tools overrides, splits HTTP-only from universal deny constants, adds MCP tests, and adds an axios release-age exclusion.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. On current main, createPluginToolsMcpHandlers filters ownerOnly tools but otherwise lists and dispatches all remaining tools, so a non-ownerOnly exec-style tool supplied to the MCP bridge would be exposed; no live MCP run was performed in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual security review is needed because the branch is stale/conflicting, changes MCP default tool exposure policy, lacks real behavior proof, and automation cannot provide the contributor's real MCP setup evidence.
